steps: On a clean Windows 11 VM, download the signed Toaster .exe and .msi from a tagged release.
Run the .exe; confirm SmartScreen either accepts silently or shows the 'Verified Publisher' dialog (not 'Unknown Publisher').
Run the .msi installer; confirm the installer launches without warning and Toaster installs.
After implementation, run: pwsh scripts/sign-windows.ps1 --verify-only --file <published .exe>
Confirm exit code 0 and that signtool verify /pa /v output reports a valid Authenticode signature with timestamping.
Repeat against the published .msi.
On a clean macOS VM (current Sonoma+), download the signed/stapled Toaster .dmg from a tagged release.
Open the .dmg and drag Toaster.app to /Applications.
Launch Toaster.app; confirm Gatekeeper accepts and no 'unidentified developer' prompt appears.
After implementation, run: bash scripts/sign-macos.sh --verify-only <Toaster.app>
Confirm exit code 0 and that codesign --verify --deep --strict reports no errors.
Run spctl --assess --type execute -v <Toaster.app> and confirm exit 0 with 'accepted' / 'source=Notarized Developer ID'.
After implementation, run: bash scripts/sign-linux.sh --verify-only Toaster.AppImage
Confirm exit code 0 and that gpg --verify reports 'Good signature' from the maintainer key.
Repeat against the published .deb.
Open .github/workflows/release.yml and find the signing job.
Confirm it has 'if: startsWith(github.ref, ''refs/tags/v'')' or equivalent tag guard.
Confirm every secret reference uses '${{ secrets.<NAME> }}' syntax and no literal cert/key material is present.
Run: rg -i 'MII[A-Za-z0-9+/]{20,}|-----BEGIN (CERTIFICATE|PRIVATE KEY|PGP PRIVATE)' . --glob '!node_modules' --glob '!target' --glob '!dist'
Confirm zero matches outside the maintainer-pubkey file (docs/maintainer.gpg is the public key only and contains 'BEGIN PGP PUBLIC KEY' not PRIVATE).
Push a non-tagged commit to a feature branch.
Open the resulting GitHub Actions run.
Confirm the signing job is shown as 'skipped' (not run).
